description: Alerting rules for the GCP IAM Privilege Escalation Chain scenario (Cloud Audit)

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP IAM PrivEsc Chain — Service Account Key Creation
relatedDashboards: GCP IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
GCP Cloud Audit Logs — API Activity
rules.investigationGuide:
# CreateAccessKey (Non-Admin)

A non-admin minted a new access key.

## Triage
1. Check caller vs target: `FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.action == "CreateAccessKey" | KEEP @timestamp, user.name, aws.cloudtrail.request_parameters, source.ip, user_agent.original | SORT @timestamp DESC`
2. If caller != target user, high signal
3. Is target user privileged?

## Escalate when
- Caller != target user
- Target is a service account
- Source IP outside corp/VPN

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP IAM PrivEsc Chain — SetIamPolicy
relatedDashboards: GCP IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
GCP Cloud Audit Logs — API Activity
rules.investigationGuide:
# AdministratorAccess Policy Attached

A non-admin attached AdministratorAccess — very high signal.

## Triage
1. Confirm: `FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.action == "AttachUserPolicy" AND aws.cloudtrail.request_parameters LIKE "*AdministratorAccess*" | KEEP @timestamp, user.name, aws.cloudtrail.request_parameters, source.ip | SORT @timestamp DESC`
2. Detach the policy immediately
3. Check caller's full activity timeline

## Escalate when
- **Always page security on-call.** Admin-attach by non-admin is rare and high-confidence.

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP IAM PrivEsc Chain — GenerateAccessToken
relatedDashboards: GCP IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
GCP Cloud Audit Logs — API Activity
rules.investigationGuide:
# AssumeRole With Privilege-Escalation Tactic

A role assumption was tagged as privilege escalation.

## Triage
1. Check tactic: `FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.action == "AssumeRole" AND threat.tactic.name IS NOT NULL | KEEP @timestamp, user.name, aws.cloudtrail.request_parameters, threat.tactic.name, source.ip | SORT @timestamp DESC`
2. Trace what the assumed role did next
3. Revoke the session if suspicious

## Escalate when
- Assumed role has admin or production access
- Multiple AssumeRole events from same caller

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] GCP IAM PrivEsc Chain — High Volume of Sensitive IAM API Calls
relatedDashboards: GCP IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
GCP Cloud Audit Logs — API Activity
rules.investigationGuide:
# High Volume of Dangerous IAM Actions

**Apex rule — treat as confirmed privilege escalation.**

## Triage
1. Identify principal: `FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.action IN ("CreateAccessKey", "AttachUserPolicy", "AssumeRole") | STATS dangerous = COUNT(*) BY user.name, event.action | SORT dangerous DESC`
2. Reconstruct full timeline on the linked dashboard
3. Disable the principal, rotate keys, detach policies

## Escalate when
- **Always.** This is the apex rule of the IAM PrivEsc chain.
